summary: Quick reference for CPU scheduling goals and common policies.
examples: Compare FCFS and Round Robin on short jobs.
Explain why SJF minimizes average waiting time.
Pick a policy for interactive vs batch workloads.
diagrams: Timeline comparing FCFS, SJF, and Round Robin
content:
## Overview
Scheduling decides which process runs on the CPU. The policy affects throughput, responsiveness, and fairness.

### Key goals
- Low waiting time
- High throughput
- Fairness and responsiveness

### Common policies
- FCFS: simple, can cause long waits
- SJF: optimal average waiting time, needs job length estimate
- Round Robin: good for interactive systems
- Priority: flexible but can starve low-priority jobs
title: Guide 4: Scheduling Essentials
steps: Pick metrics: waiting time, turnaround, response.
Choose a policy that matches the workload.
Simulate on sample processes to validate.
Watch for starvation and apply aging if needed.
